title: SSC CGL 2026 Sectional Timing Tier I 3 3 20260522

questions.question: If all the corporate firms across the world allow their employees to work remotely, then this would help job seekers living in rural communities and relatively smaller towns. The conclusion drawn depends on which of the following assumptions?
options: It would be easier for the people from smaller towns to grab such job opportunities which are beyond their technical skills and abilities.
One of the benefits of working from home is having access to a broader range of job opportunities that aren't limited by geographic location.
It is always uneasy for job seekers having a rural background to shift to an urban lifestyle.
The corporate firms would be benefited by hiring more and more employees from smaller towns.
questions.explanation: The given statement talks about how working remotely will help in seeking jobs for people living in rural areas and small towns. This conclusion assumes, that remote work does not have geographical limitation at all and all can have access to work despite their location. This is portrayed in option b). Option a) brings in the technical skills' concept to light, which isn't related to the conclusion drawn. Option c) takes us to another aspect that talks about difficulties faced in shifting from rural to urban lifestyle. The word 'always' makes it an assumption, that may or may not be related to working remotely. Option d) talks about the benefits to the corporate firms; this fails to take into account 'the jobseekers' living in small towns and rural areas.

questions.question: The difference between the greatest decimal number and smallest decimal number, 0.8678, 0.09875, 0.05989, 0.7856 is:
questions.explanation: The greatest decimal number = 0.8678 The smallest decimal number = 0.05989 So, the required difference = 0.8678 - 0.05989 = 0.80791

questions.question: A number is 25 more than ⅗ of other number and the sum of the numbers is 105. Find the LCM of the numbers.
questions.explanation: Let a number = 5m Other number = ⅗ * 5m + 25 = 3m + 25 5m + 3m + 25 = 105 m = 10 So, the numbers are 50, 55. LCM of 50 and 55 = 550

questions.question: In each cluster of five integers, three clusters follow a similar pattern; one cluster does not. Identify the odd cluster.
questions.explanation: A: 10 - 14 are consecutive. B: 12,13,15,16,17 - missing 14; not consecutive. C: 5 - 9 are consecutive D: 20 - 24 are consecutive. Thus, Option B is odd.

questions.question: The sequence of folding a piece of paper and the manner in which the folded paper has been cut is shown in the following figures. Select the option that would most clearly resemble the unfolded form of paper. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p28857/image025.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p28857/image026.png)
questions.explanation: Figure IV would resemble the correct unfolded form of a paper after cutting. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p28857/image027.png)

questions.question: Select the option that shows the given words arranged as they appear in an English dictionary. 1) Aviation 2) Aversion 3) Avenue 4) Avatar 5) Avarice 6) Average
questions.explanation: 5) Avarice 4) Avatar 3) Avenue 6) Average 2) Aversion 1) Aviation So, the dictionary order is (5), (4), (3), (6), (2), (1).

questions.question: Blade is to knife as ______ is to saw.
questions.explanation: The blade is the cutting component of a knife. The teeth are the cutting components of a saw. Handles, sheaths, and grips are not the analogous cutting parts. Therefore, option C is correct.

questions.question: Ajay has the following profile: Education: B.Sc. with 66% and M.Sc. in Chemistry (passed) Experience: 1 year in the relevant field Age: 24 years Preference: Open to probationary roles Eligibility Criteria for the Position: Minimum Age: 24 years; Maximum Age: 29 years Educational Qualification: At least M.Sc. in Chemistry Experience: Minimum 2 years for direct selection Probationary Roles: Open to candidates with 6 - 18 months' experience who satisfy age and qualification Minimum Marks in B.Sc.: 65% Decision: Based on the provided information, what is the most appropriate decision regarding Ajay's eligibility for the position?
options: Candidate is not to be selected
Candidate is eligible only if he accepts a probationary role
questions.explanation: Age 24 satisfies the band; qualification M.Sc. satisfied; B.Sc. 66% ≥ 65%. Experience 1 year < 2 years for direct selection. Probation allowed for 6 - 18 months' experience with age and qualification satisfied. Decision => eligible only on probation.

questions.question: Select the operator sequence (left to right) to make the statement true:27 ? 3 ? 4 ? 39
questions.explanation: On checking option (b) 27 + 3 X 4 = 27 + 12 = 39 Other choices fail to produce 39.

questions.question: Select the combination of numbers so that letters arranged accordingly will form a meaningful word. 1:L 2:V 3:G 4:S 5:O 6:E
questions.explanation: (a) 3-5-1-2-6-4: G-O-L-V-E-S (Not a meaningful word) (b) 3-1-2-5-6-4: G-L-V-O-E-S (Not a meaningful word) (c) 5-3-1-2-6-4: O-G-L-V-E-S (Not a meaningful word) (d) 3-1-5-2-6-4: G-L-O-V-E-S (Meaningful word) The correct combination is 3-1-5-2-6-4, which forms the word GLOVES.

questions.question: Select the number from among the given options that can replace the question mark (?) in the following series. 729, 512, 343, 216, 125, ?
questions.explanation: These are descending perfect cubes. 729 = 9³, 512 = 8³, 343 = 7³, 216 = 6³, 125 = 5³. Next = 4³ = 64.

questions.question: Select the letter-cluster from among the given options that can replace the question mark (?) in the following series. AZA, BYB, CXC, DWD, ?
questions.explanation: Outer letters are the same in each term and go A, B, C, D → next E. Middle letters go Z, Y, X, W → next V. So the next cluster is EVE.

questions.question: Along a straight corridor, there are some flagpoles in a line. Pole A is 16th from the left end and Pole B is 9th from the right end. Pole C is exactly midway between A and B, and C is 20th from the left end. How many flagpoles are there?
questions.explanation: Let N = total flagpoles Position of B from the left = N − 9 + 1 = N − 8 Midway means C's position = (position of A + position of B) ÷ 2 20 = (16 + (N − 8)) ÷ 2 40 = 16 + N − 8 40 = N + 8 N = 32

questions.question: Usha started walking straight from her house. She turned 6 times left during her journey. Finally, she is facing towards north. In which direction did she start walking from her house? (She took no right turn)
questions.explanation: Usha started walking straight from her house. She turned 6 times left during her journey. Finally, she is facing towards north. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30320/image011.jpg) So, Usha started in the opposite direction of North. So, she started walking in the south direction.

questions.question: In the given question, there is a certain relationship between two given letter pairs on one side of (::) and a pair that is given on another side of (::) where another pair is to be found from the given alternatives, having the same relationship with this pair as the pairs of the given pair bear. Choose the best alternative. AJQM : DFTL :: PXEV : ?
questions.explanation: We know that, AlphabetsABCDEFGHIJKLM Positional value12345678910111213 AlphabetsZYXWVUTSRQPON Positional value26252423222120191817161514 Here, the logic is: A+3= D; J-4= F; Q+3= T; M-1= L Similarly, P+3= S; X-4= T; E+3= H; V-1= U.

questions.question: In a certain code language, 'Knowledge creates strong foundation' is coded as '6294', 'Strong mind creates victory' is coded as '9827' and 'Mind needs strong efforts' is coded as '8591'. If building foundation is coded as '40', What will be the code for 'Great Knowledge'?
questions.explanation: In a certain code language, 'Knowledge creates strong foundation' is coded as '6294' 'Strong mind creates victory' is coded as '9827' 'Mind needs strong efforts' is coded as '8591'. The code for 'Strong' = 9 The code for 'Creates' = 2 The code for 'Mind' = 8 The code for 'Knowledge' / 'Foundation' = 6 / 4 If building foundation is coded as '40'- Thus, the code for 'Foundation' = 4 The code for 'Knowledge' = 6 So, the code for 'Great knowledge' = 63

questions.question: Find the missing number: 10 : 728 :: ? : 1727

Six persons- Dustin, Lucas, Will, Mike, Steve and Robin are sitting in a linear row facing the south direction. Mike sits third to the right of Lucas. Will sits second to the right of Steve. Mike sits immediate left of Dustin. Who among the following sits second to the left of Robin?
questions.explanation: Mike sits third to the right of Lucas. Mike sits immediate left of Dustin. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30405/image012.png) Will sits second to the right of Steve. Thus case 2 is invalid. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30405/image013.png) Now, only Robin is remaining. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30405/image014.png) Lucas sits second to the left of Robin.

questions.question: Dhami said to Shilpa that, "Your husband's only sister's mother is my son's maternal grandmother". How is Dhami related to Shilpa?
questions.explanation: Dhami said to Shilpa that, "Your husband's only sister's mother is my son's maternal grandmother". ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30405/image032.png) Dhami is the sister-in-law of Shilpa.

questions.question: Ree is the father-in-law of Tee. Kee is the brother of Lee. Zee is the sister of Nee. Lee is the daughter of Dee. Nee is the mother of Kee. Zee is the spouse of Tee. What is the relation between Ree and Dee?
options: Ree is the father-in-law of Dee.
Ree is the uncle of Dee.
Ree is the brother-in-law of Dee.
Ree is the grandfather of Dee.
questions.explanation: Ree is the father-in-law of Tee. Kee is the brother of Lee. Zee is the sister of Nee. Lee is the daughter of Dee. Nee is the mother of Kee. Zee is the spouse of Tee. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30405/image036.png) Ree is the father-in-law of Dee.

questions.question: If '-' is coded as 'x', '÷' is coded as '+', 'x' is coded as '÷' and '+' is coded as '-', then find the value of the following expression. [(450 + 225) x 15] - (64 ÷ 39) = ?
Select the numbers from among the given options that can replace the question marks (?) in the following series. 3, 13, 40, 101, 230, ?
In a certain code language, 'BRAIN' is coded as '259261813' and 'LUCKY' is coded as '15624162'. How will 'WHITE' be coded in that language?

If in an examination the centre codes and roll numbers are formed by the initial of the State/UT followed by five digits in a fixed pattern (for example, MP95341 for Madhya Pradesh), then which of the following roll numbers is most likely to be from Himachal Pradesh?
questions.explanation: Pattern = State Initial + same five digits (95341). Himachal Pradesh code is HP, so HP95341 is correct. Other options use different state codes.

questions.question: In the following question, which one set of letters when sequentially placed at the gaps in the given letter series shall complete it? cr_st_lc_ys_al_rys_a_cr_st_l
options: y, a, s, t, c, t, m, y, a
y, a, r, t, c, t, l, y, a
y, a, r, t, d, t, l, y, a
y, a, r, t, d, t, m, y, a
questions.explanation: crystalcrystalcrystalcrystal So, option (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: The Losar festival, celebrated in Ladakh, marks the beginning of the Tibetan New Year and is traditionally observed for ______ days.
questions.explanation: Losar (derived from 'Lo' meaning year and 'Sar' meaning new) is the most significant socio-religious festival of the Buddhist community in Ladakh. Although the most intense celebrations occur during the first three days, the festival is traditionally observed for a period of 15 days. Further Insights: Historical Context in Ladakh: Interestingly, Losar in Ladakh is often celebrated two months ahead of the standard Tibetan New Year. Legend traces this back to the 17th-century King Jamyang Namgyal, who decided to celebrate the New Year early before leading a military campaign in the winter. Key Rituals: Metho Ceremony: A procession where people carry flaming torches to drive away evil spirits and bad luck from the previous year. Guthuk: A special noodle soup consumed on the eve of Losar, containing nine different ingredients. Religious Offerings: Elaborate offerings called 'Tarzok' are made in monasteries and family shrines to ensure prosperity. Significance: It symbolizes the victory of good over evil and is a time for family reunions, cleaning houses to welcome the new year, and performing traditional folk dances and songs.

questions.question: Why is the 'L1' point (Lagrangian Point 1) chosen for the Aditya-L1 mission?
options: It is the closest point to the Moon.
It allows the satellite to see the Sun without eclipses or occultation.
It is where the Earth's magnetic field is strongest.
It is the only point where a satellite can remain stationary relative to the stars.
questions.explanation: The L1 point allows the satellite to observe the Sun continuously without any eclipses or occultations. Explanation of Options: (a) is Incorrect. The L1 point of the Sun-Earth system is located about 1.5 million kilometers away from Earth toward the Sun. It has no relation to the Moon; lunar missions target the Moon's orbit or surface. (b) is Correct. Lagrangian Point 1 (L1) is a gravitationally stable spot where a satellite remains in a fixed position relative to the Sun and the Earth. Its major advantage is that the Earth never blocks the satellite's view of the Sun, ensuring 24/7 observation of solar activities. (c) is Incorrect. Earth's magnetic field is strongest near its poles and weakest as one moves further away into space. L1 is located far beyond the Earth's primary magnetosphere. (d) is Incorrect. A satellite at L1 remains stationary relative to the Sun-Earth system, not the distant stars. It orbits the Sun at the same rate as the Earth. Further Insights: Aditya-L1 was launched by ISRO on September 2, 2023. It carries seven scientific payloads to study the solar corona, solar emissions, and solar winds. By staying at L1, the mission saves fuel because the combined gravitational pull of the Sun and Earth provides the necessary centripetal force for the satellite to stay in orbit.

questions.question: The Khetri belt in Rajasthan is primarily known for the extraction of which important mineral resource?
questions.explanation: The Khetri belt in Rajasthan is primarily known for the extraction of Copper. Explanation of Options: (a) is Incorrect. Bauxite (the ore for aluminium) is predominantly found in states like Odisha, Gujarat, Jharkhand, and Maharashtra. Rajasthan is not a major producer of bauxite. (b) is Incorrect. Manganese is mainly mined in Odisha, Maharashtra, Madhya Pradesh, and Karnataka. While Rajasthan has some deposits, it is not the primary mineral associated with the Khetri region. (c) is Incorrect. Coal deposits in India are concentrated in the Gondwana belt (Jharkhand, Odisha, Chhattisgarh, West Bengal) and Tertiary fields (Assam, Meghalaya). Rajasthan produces Lignite (brown coal), but the Khetri belt is specifically a metallic mineral zone. (d) is Correct. The Khetri Copper Belt in the Jhunjhunu district of Rajasthan is one of India's few major copper-producing regions. It is an 80 km long belt famous for its high-quality copper ore and is home to the Khetri Copper Complex. Further Insights: · Hindustan Copper Limited (HCL): The Khetri Copper Complex is a unit of HCL, a public sector undertaking. It was established to develop the copper deposits in this region. · Historical Significance: Copper mining in Khetri dates back to ancient times; it is believed that the people of the Indus Valley Civilization obtained copper from this region to make tools and ornaments. · Secondary Minerals: Apart from copper, the ore in this region often contains traces of other valuable metals like gold, silver, and selenium, which are recovered as by-products during the refining process. · Geology: The copper mineralization in Khetri is associated with the Aravalli range, one of the oldest fold mountain systems in the world, which is exceptionally rich in non-ferrous minerals.

questions.question: Which country partners with India in the Maitri military exercise aimed at defence cooperation?
questions.explanation: · The 11th edition of the India-Sri Lanka Joint Military Exercise, Mitra Shakti XI-2025, successfully concluded in November 2025 at the Foreign Training Node in Belagavi, Karnataka, focusing on rehearsing Sub-Conventional Operations and counter-terrorism under UN mandates, enhancing interoperability, and utilizing drones/C-UAS for peacekeeping. · The exercise involved contingents from the Rajput Regiment (India) and Gajaba Regiment (Sri Lanka), strengthening defence cooperation and military ties between the two nations.

questions.question: The speed of light in vacuum is approximately:
questions.explanation: The speed of light in vacuum is approximately 299,792 kilometres per second, which is commonly rounded to 300,000 km/s for calculation purposes. It is a fundamental constant of nature, denoted by c. Light travels fastest in vacuum and slows down when passing through media like air, water, or glass.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding Mauryan Pillar Architecture: 1) The shafts of these pillars were usually made of multiple pieces of sandstone joined together. 2) They were often topped with a capital featuring a sacred animal and a dharma-chakra. Which of the statements above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement II is correct. Statement I is incorrect because Mauryan pillars were monolithic, meaning they were carved from a single piece of stone. Explanation of Statements: I. is Incorrect. A defining characteristic of Mauryan pillars (especially those commissioned by Ashoka) is that they are monolithic. The entire shaft was carved from a single block of stone, typically Chunar sandstone. This is a major point of contrast with the Achaemenian (Persian) pillars, which were constructed using multiple stone segments joined together. II. is Correct. Mauryan pillars were designed with a sophisticated structural hierarchy. The shaft was topped by a capital, which usually featured a realistically carved sacred animal (such as a lion, bull, or elephant) standing on an abacus. In many instances, most notably the Sarnath Lion Capital, the structure was crowned with a dharma-chakra (Wheel of Law), symbolizing the spread of Dhamma. Further Insights: Stone Source: Most Mauryan pillars were made of grey-spotted Chunar sandstone, quarried near Varanasi. Mauryan Polish: These pillars are famous for their unique, mirror-like "lustrous polish," a technique that became a hallmark of Mauryan craftsmanship and has largely been lost to time. Purpose: These pillars were not part of buildings but were free-standing structures inscribed with edicts to communicate the King's message of social and religious harmony to the public. Components: A typical pillar consists of four parts: the monolithic shaft, the bell-shaped lotus base, the abacus (circular or rectangular base), and the animal capital.

questions.question: Who wrote the influential book "India: A Million Mutinies Now"?
questions.explanation: V.S. Naipaul wrote India: A Million Mutinies Now in 1990. It is the final part of his famous "India" trilogy. Unlike his earlier, more critical books on India, this work is often seen as a more optimistic and empathetic narrative, where he travels across the country interviewing people from various walks of life to understand the growing complexities of post-independence India. Further Insights: The Trilogy: India: A Million Mutinies Now concludes a series that began with An Area of Darkness (1964) and continued with India: A Wounded Civilization (1977). Theme: The "Million Mutinies" in the title refers to the countless small-scale social, political, and religious awakenings and assertions of identity across India that Naipaul witnessed during his travels. He viewed these not as a sign of collapse, but as a sign of a society's growth and democratization. Awards: V.S. Naipaul, a Trinidadian-British writer of Indian descent, was awarded the Nobel Prize in Literature in 2001. Literary Style: The book is a masterpiece of travel writing and oral history, relying heavily on the voices of ordinary Indians - from Dalit activists to stockbrokers and religious leaders - to paint a panoramic picture of the nation.

questions.question: Which Mughal official was responsible for the maintenance of law and order in the cities during the reign of Humayun and Akbar?
questions.explanation: The Kotwal was the official responsible for the maintenance of law and order in the cities during the Mughal period. Explanation of Options: (a) is Incorrect. The Mir Bakshi was the head of the military department and was responsible for the Mansabdari system and intelligence gathering, not specifically city policing. (b) is Correct. The Kotwal was the chief of the city police. Under the Mughals, his duties were extensive: he maintained law and order, kept track of newcomers, acted as a census officer, and supervised the local markets to prevent hoarding or cheating in weights and measures. (c) is Incorrect. The Qazi was a judicial officer responsible for interpreting Islamic law and delivering judgments in civil and criminal cases. (d) is Incorrect. The Amalguzar (also known as the Amil) was a revenue official responsible for the assessment and collection of land revenue in the Sarkars (districts). Further Insights: Ain-i-Akbari: Abul Fazl's Ain-i-Akbari describes the Kotwal as an ideal officer who should be fearless, tireless, and well-acquainted with the residents of his jurisdiction. 24-Hour Vigilance: The Kotwal was also responsible for organizing night watches (chowkidari) and ensuring that the city gates were closed and guarded at appropriate times.

questions.question: UIDAI has empowered Aadhaar number holders of India with the unique identity and a digital platform to authenticate anytime, anywhere. Which of the following statements is/are correct regarding UIDAI's recent steps towards Aadhaar number deactivation in case of death? I. UIDAI deactivates Aadhaar numbers only after receiving biometric confirmation of death from the family. II. UIDAI launched a service called "Reporting of Death of a Family Member" on the myAadhaar portal in June 2025. III. UIDAI is sharing data of Aadhaar holders above 100 years with State Governments for verification of their living status.
questions.explanation: · Statement I is incorrect because UIDAI does not require biometric confirmation of death from the family. Instead, the process involves submission of Aadhaar number, death registration number, and demographic details for verification. · Statement II is correct: UIDAI launched the 'Reporting of Death of a Family Member' service on the myAadhaar portal on 9th June 2025. · Statement III is correct: UIDAI has initiated a pilot project where it shares demographic data of Aadhaar holders aged 100 with State Governments for verification. UIDAI has empowered Aadhaar number holders of India with the unique identity and a digital platform to authenticate anytime, anywhere. · Aadhaar number is a unique 12-digit digital identity for the residents of India and NRIs. The 12-digit Aadhaar number is a random number generated without the use of any intelligence and, hence, not all 12-digit numbers are Aadhaar numbers. No Aadhaar number is ever re-assigned to another individual. · However, in case of the death of a person, it is essential that his/her Aadhaar number is deactivated to prevent identity fraud and unauthorised usage of such Aadhaar number. It is also important to validate the status of the deceased Aadhaar number holders prior to deactivation of their Aadhaar numbers, as it may have widespread ramifications for them. · Therefore, in order to maintain the continued accuracy of the Aadhaar database, UIDAI has proactively taken the following measures to obtain death records from various sources and, deactivate Aadhaar numbers after due validation: · Recently, UIDAI requested the Registrar General of India (RGI) to share death records linked with Aadhaar numbers. RGI has, till date, provided approximately 1.55 crore death records from 24 States/UTs using the Civil Registration System (CRS). After due validation, around 1.17 crore Aadhaar numbers have been deactivated. A similar exercise is continuing with non-CRS States/UTs. About 6.7 lakh death records have been received so far, and deactivation is in progress. · UIDAI has launched a new service - 'Reporting of Death of a Family Member' - on the myAadhaar Portal on 9th June 2025 for deaths registered in 24 States/UTs presently using Civil Registration System. This portal allows individuals to report the death of their family members. The family member, after authenticating himself/herself, is required to provide Aadhaar number and Death Registration Number along with other demographic details of the deceased person on the portal. After due process of validation of the information submitted by the family member, further action is taken for the deactivation, or otherwise, of the Aadhaar number of the deceased person. The process of integration of remaining States/UTs with the portal is currently underway. · Besides above, UIDAI is also exploring the possibility of sourcing death records from banks and other Aadhaar ecosystem entities maintaining such information. · UIDAI is also taking support of State Governments in identifying the deceased Aadhaar number holders. As a pilot, demographic details of Aadhaar number holders aged more than 100 years are being shared with State Governments to verify whether the Aadhaar number holder is alive. Upon receipt of such verification report, necessary validation will be carried out before deactivating such Aadhaar number. · In order to prevent unauthorised usage of Aadhaar number of any family member after their death, it is recommended that Aadhaar number holders report death of their family members on myAadhaar Portal after obtaining their death certificate from the death registering authorities.

questions.question: In Hindustani classical music, "Andolan" refers to:
options: Slow and gentle oscillation of a note
questions.explanation: In Hindustani classical music, "Andolan" refers to the slow and gentle oscillation of a note. Explanation of Options: (a) is Incorrect. Fast oscillations are typically referred to as Taan, Murki, or certain types of Gamak. (b) is Correct. Andolan is a specific type of ornamentation (Gamak) where a note (swara) is gently swung back and forth within a very small range of its pitch. The singer or instrumentalist touches the microtones (shrutis) adjacent to the main note, but never moves far enough to hit a different note. (c) is Incorrect. A sudden jump between octaves or notes is generally called an Interval or a Meend (if glided). (d) is Incorrect. Rhythmic variations of tempo are related to Layakari. Further Insights: Raga Application: Certain ragas are defined by their specific use of Andolan. For example, in Raga Darbari Kanhada, the note Ga (Gandhar) must be performed with a heavy, slow Andolan to achieve its characteristic somber mood. Emotional Depth: Andolan is used to provide depth, gravity, and emotional resonance (Bhava) to a performance.

questions.question: In the PMFBY (Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana), what is the fixed premium rate to be paid by farmers for all Kharif crops?
questions.explanation: The fixed premium rate to be paid by farmers for all Kharif crops under the PMFBY is 2.0%. Under the Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY), the premium rates are capped at a very low level to reduce the financial burden on farmers. For all Kharif crops (summer/monsoon crops), the premium to be paid by the farmer is fixed at 2.0% of the sum insured or the actuarial rate, whichever is less. Further Insights: Government Subsidy: The difference between the actuarial premium rate (the actual market cost of the insurance) and the rate paid by the farmers is shared equally by the Central and State Governments as a subsidy. Objective: Launched in 2016, PMFBY aims to provide a comprehensive insurance cover against the failure of the crop, thus helping in stabilizing the income of the farmers and encouraging them to adopt innovative practices. Coverage: The scheme covers all stages of the crop cycle, including pre-sowing to post-harvest losses due to non-preventable natural risks such as drought, floods, landslides, pests, and diseases. Revamped PMFBY (2.0): In 2020, the scheme was made voluntary for all farmers (earlier it was mandatory for loanee farmers). It also increased the flexibility for states to customize the scheme according to their local climatic risks.

questions.question: Which of the following statements regarding the Arjuna Award is incorrect?
options: It is the second-highest sporting honour of India.
It was the highest sporting honour of India before the introduction of the Major Dhyan Chand Khel Ratna Award.
It is awarded annually by the Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports.
It is given exclusively for lifetime contribution to sports.
questions.explanation: · The Arjuna Award is given for outstanding performance in sports over a period of four years, not exclusively for lifetime contribution to sports. · The Arjuna Award is India's second-highest sporting honour, after the Major Dhyan Chand Khel Ratna Award. · The award is given annually by the Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports for outstanding international-level sports performance over a four-year period, along with leadership and sportsmanship qualities. Further insights: · Instituted in 1961, it is named after Arjuna from the Mahabharata, symbolising dedication, discipline, and focus. · Before the introduction of the Khel Ratna in 1991 - 92, the Arjuna Award was India's highest sporting honour. · Nominations are received from recognised sports bodies, government authorities, and previous awardees, and recipients are selected by a ministry-appointed committee. · As of 2020, the award includes a bronze statuette of Arjuna, a certificate, ceremonial dress, and a cash prize of ₹15 lakh. · Over time, the award has been expanded and revised to include more disciplines, indigenous games, para-sports, and team events. · The latest 2018 guidelines limit the award to major international sports events (such as the Olympics, Asian Games, World Championships, and World Cups), recommend a maximum of 15 awards per year, and ensure representation for physically challenged athletes.

questions.question: The Supreme Court of India is the ______ interpreter of the Constitution.
questions.explanation: The Supreme Court of India is the final interpreter and the guardian of the Indian Constitution. While other bodies like the Parliament can amend the Constitution, it is the Supreme Court that has the ultimate authority to explain what its provisions mean and to ensure that no law violates its "Basic Structure." Why is the Supreme Court the "Final" Interpreter? Judicial Review: Under the power of judicial review, the Supreme Court can examine any law passed by the Parliament or any order issued by the Government. If it finds them inconsistent with the Constitution, it can declare them null and void. Binding Decisions: According to Article 141 of the Constitution, the law declared by the Supreme Court is binding on all other courts within the territory of India. Basic Structure Doctrine: Established in the landmark Kesavananda Bharati case (1973), the Court ruled that the Parliament cannot alter certain fundamental features of the Constitution. The Court itself decides which features qualify as "basic." Writ Jurisdiction: Under Article 32, citizens can directly approach the Supreme Court for the protection of their Fundamental Rights, making it the "sentinel on the qui vive" (watchful guardian).

questions.question: Which of the following industries was reserved exclusively for the public sector under Industrial Policy Resolution(IPR), 1956?
questions.explanation: Under the Industrial Policy Resolution (IPR) of 1956, which is often called the "Economic Constitution of India," industries were classified into three categories or "Schedules." Steel (specifically listed as "Iron and Steel") was placed in Schedule A, meaning its future development was the exclusive responsibility of the State. Classification under IPR, 1956 The resolution aimed to establish a socialistic pattern of society by giving the public sector a dominant role in the economy. Category Description Number of Industries Examples Schedule A Exclusive Public Sector - Owned and managed solely by the government. 17 Steel, Arms & Ammunition, Atomic Energy, Railways, Mineral Oils, Coal. Schedule B Mixed Sector - State would take the lead, but the private sector could supplement. 12 Aluminum, Machine Tools, Fertilizers, Synthetic Rubber. Schedule C Private Sector - Open to private enterprises, but regulated through government licensing. Rest Cotton textiles, Sugar, Cement, Consumer goods.

questions.question: Under the Kinetic Theory of Gases, an "Ideal Gas" is one where:
options: Particles have high attractive forces at high pressure.
The volume of the gas particles is significant.
All collisions between particles and the container walls are perfectly elastic.
The gas can be liquefied at very low temperatures.
questions.explanation: In an ideal gas, all collisions between particles and the container walls are perfectly elastic. Explanation of Options: (a) is Incorrect. A fundamental postulate of the Kinetic Theory for an ideal gas is that there are no attractive or repulsive forces between the gas molecules. Large attractive forces are a characteristic of "Real Gases" at high pressure. (b) is Incorrect. For an ideal gas, the actual volume of the gas particles is assumed to be negligible (zero) compared to the total volume of the container. (c) is Correct. "Perfectly elastic" means that there is no net loss of kinetic energy during collisions. The energy is transferred between particles, but the total kinetic energy of the system remains constant at a constant temperature. (d) is Incorrect. Ideal gases cannot be liquefied. Liquefaction requires intermolecular attractive forces to pull molecules together into a liquid state; since ideal gases are assumed to have no such forces, they remain gaseous regardless of temperature or pressure. Further Insights: The Ideal Gas Law is expressed as PV=nRT Real gases behave most like ideal gases under conditions of low pressure and high temperature, where the particles are far apart and moving too fast for attractive forces to take effect.

questions.question: Which film was awarded as the Best Feature Film, recognized for its powerful narrative and direction at the 71st National Film Awards in September 2025?
questions.explanation: · 12th Fail has been honoured with the Best Feature Film award at the 71st National Film Awards. · The 71st National Film Awards lit up Vigyan Bhawan, New Delhi, with moments of pride, applause and standing ovations as the Hon'ble President of India, Smt. Droupadi Murmu, conferred honours on the finest talents of Indian cinema. · The winners of the 71st National Film Awards for the year 2023 were announced by the respective juries. This year, the awards saw 332 entries in the feature film category, 115 in non-feature films, 27 books, and 16 critics' submissions. · Flowering Man won the Best Non-Feature Film award, while God Vulture and Human was recognised as the Best Documentary. Both films earned their first National Film Award. · Shah Rukh Khan (Jawan) and Vikrant Massey (12th Fail) have been awarded the Best Actor award. This is the first National Film Award of Sharukh Khan in his career. · Rani Mukerji was honoured with the Best Actress award for her powerful performance in Mrs. Chatterjee Vs Norway. This is also her first-ever National Film Award. · Veteran actor Vijayaraghavan and Muthupettai Somu Bhaskar were awarded Best Supporting Actor. · The announcement was made by Shri Ashutosh Gowariker (Feature Film Jury Chairperson), Shri P. Sheshadri (Non-Feature Film Jury Chairperson), and Dr. Ajay Nagabhushan MN, Joint Secretary (Films). Ms. Mattu J. P. Singh, Director General, PIB, was also present on the occasion.

questions.question: Read the below statements marked as Assertion (A) and Reason (R). Mark the correct option: Assertion (A): Bimbisara followed a policy of matrimonial alliances to expand the Kingdom of Magadha. Reason (R): His marriage to the princess of Kosala brought the territory of Kashi as a dowry, providing significant revenue.
options: Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).
Both (A) and (R) are true but (R) is NOT the correct explanation of (A).
questions.explanation: Assertion (A) is Correct. Bimbisara, the founder of the Haryanka dynasty, was a brilliant diplomat. He recognized that Magadha could not expand solely through warfare. He utilized matrimonial alliances to strengthen his political position and neutralize potential rivals, thereby laying the foundation for the Magadhan Empire. Reason (R) is Correct. His most significant marriage was with Kosala-devi, the sister of King Prasenajit of Kosala. As a part of this marriage settlement, the village of Kashi was granted to Bimbisara as "pin-money" (dowry). This territory yielded a massive annual revenue of one lakh coins, which significantly boosted Magadha's treasury and strategic influence. Further Insights: Other Alliances: Bimbisara also married Chellana, a Lichchhavi princess from Vaishali, and Khema, the daughter of the King of Madra (modern-day Punjab). These alliances protected his northern and western frontiers. Contemporary of Buddha: Bimbisara was a contemporary of both Gautama Buddha and Vardhamana Mahavira. He is often credited with the first organized administrative system in ancient India.

questions.question: What is the maximum duration of a "Golden Score" (overtime) period in a standard Judo contest?
options: No time limit (until a score/penalty occurs)
questions.explanation: In a standard Judo contest, the Golden Score period has no time limit. While some age-specific or local variations might impose a time limit for safety or scheduling, the official International Judo Federation (IJF) rules for senior competitions do not set a maximum duration for overtime. In the past, Judo used a system called Hantei (judge's decision by flags), but this was replaced by the infinite Golden Score to ensure a winner is determined by technical action. If a match ends in a draw after the regulation four minutes, it immediately enters the Golden Score phase. The clock is reset, and the contest continues indefinitely until one judoka achieves a score (Waza-ari or Ippon) or until one judoka receives a third penalty (Shido), resulting in disqualification (Hansoku-make). Further Insights: Carry-over: All scores and penalties (Shidos) accumulated during the regular four-minute match carry over into the Golden Score period. Physical Endurance: Because there is no time limit, some high-level matches have been known to last over 10 to 15 minutes, making it an extreme test of physical and mental endurance. Winning Criteria: The very first point awarded ends the match instantly. However, a Shido (minor penalty) does not end the match unless it is the athlete's third and final penalty. Tactical Shift: Athletes often change their strategy in Golden Score, becoming either more aggressive to find a quick score or more defensive to avoid a tie-breaking penalty.

questions.question: Read the below statements marked as Assertion (A) and Reason (R). Mark the correct option: Assertion (A): The Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Samhita (BNSS) aims to modernize the criminal justice system in India. Reason (R): It allows for the use of technology in trials and expands the scope of summary trials to speed up justice.
options: Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).
Both (A) and (R) are true but (R) is NOT the explanation.
questions.explanation: Assertion (A) is Correct: The Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Samhita (BNSS) was enacted to replace the Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC), 1973. Its primary objective is to move away from colonial-era laws and create a procedural framework that is more suited to modern Indian society and contemporary technological advancements. Reason (R) is Correct: The BNSS introduces several provisions to modernize the system, such as allowing for the recording of statements via video conferencing, permitting the filing of FIRs electronically (e-FIR), and mandating the use of forensics for crimes punishable by seven years or more. By expanding the scope of summary trials (for offenses punishable by up to three years), it significantly reduces the burden on courts and speeds up the delivery of justice. Since these specific technological and procedural improvements define "modernization," R correctly explains A. Further Insights: Date of Implementation: The BNSS, along with the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS) and Bharatiya Sakshya Adhiniyam (BSA), came into effect on July 1, 2024. Zero FIR: The BNSS formally incorporates the concept of "Zero FIR," allowing a person to file a complaint at any police station regardless of where the crime occurred. Time-bound Trials: It sets specific timelines for various stages of the legal process, such as requiring judgments to be delivered within 30 days of the completion of arguments.

questions.question: Which of the following is a key function of the government in an economy?
options: Framing laws and ensuring economic justice
Only controlling prices of luxury items
questions.explanation: In any modern economy, the government plays a multifaceted role to ensure that markets function fairly and that the benefits of growth reach all sections of society. Key Functions of the Government The government does not just exist to produce goods; it serves as the referee and strategist of the economic system. Its core functions include: Establishing the Legal Framework: The government frames laws that protect property rights and enforce contracts. Without these "rules of the game," businesses and consumers would not have the confidence to trade. Ensuring Economic Justice: Through redistribution, the government uses progressive taxation (taxing the wealthy more) to fund welfare programs, healthcare, and education for the less fortunate. This helps reduce income inequality. Maintaining Competition: Governments enforce "Anti-Trust" or "Competition Laws" to prevent a single company from becoming a monopoly and charging unfair prices. Stabilizing the Economy: Using Fiscal Policy (spending and taxes) and Monetary Policy (interest rates), the government works to control inflation and prevent high unemployment during recessions. Providing Public Goods: Certain services like national defense, street lighting, and public roads are provided by the government because private companies cannot easily charge individuals for their use.

questions.question: Match the following folk dances with their state of origin: Dance State A) Ghoomari) Punjab B) Bihuii) Rajasthan C) Giddaiii) Assam D) Lavaniiv) Maharashtra
questions.explanation: A-ii is Correct. Ghoomar is the most iconic folk dance of Rajasthan. It was originally developed by the Bhil tribe to worship Goddess Saraswati and was later adopted by the Rajput royalty. The dance is characterized by women wearing flowing ghagras (skirts) that swirl beautifully as they pirouette in circles. B-iii is Correct. Bihu is the signature folk dance of Assam. It is performed by both men and women during the Bihu festivals, particularly the Rongali Bihu (Spring festival). The dance is known for its brisk steps, rapid hand movements, and the use of traditional instruments like the Dhol and Pepa. C-i is Correct. Gidda is a popular and vibrant folk dance of Punjab, performed exclusively by women. It is considered the feminine counterpart to the male Bhangra. The dance represents the spirit of the Punjabi people and is performed during festive occasions like Teeyan or weddings, accompanied by rhythmic clapping and boliyan (couplets). D-iv is Correct. Lavani is a traditional folk dance form of Maharashtra. It is a combination of traditional song and dance, typically performed to the enchanting beats of the Dholki. Lavani is noted for its powerful rhythm and the traditional attire of the performers, who wear nine-yard long Nauvari sarees. Further Insights: Ghoomar often involves difficult steps such as dancing on the rim of a brass plate or balancing several clay pots on the head while twirling. The Bihu dance serves as an expression of the joy of the arrival of the spring and the harvest, and its music is a major part of Assamese identity. Lavani can be categorized into two types: Nirguni Lavani (philosophical) and Shringari Lavani (sensual/romantic). The latter is more popular in the commercial theatre circuit. In Gidda, a specific circle is formed where one woman sings a boli and the others join in the chorus while dancing with great energy and grace.

questions.question: Regarding the Government of India Act, 1919, consider the following: 1) It introduced 'Diarchy' at the Central level of government. 2) It provided for the first time for the establishment of a Public Service Commission. Which of the statements is/are true?
questions.explanation: Statement II is correct. Statement I is incorrect because the Government of India Act, 1919, introduced 'Diarchy' at the Provincial level, while the Central government remained unitary. Explanation of Statements: I. is Incorrect. The Government of India Act, 1919 (also known as the Montagu-Chelmsford Reforms), introduced Diarchy (dual government) specifically in the Provinces. Under this system, provincial subjects were divided into two categories: 'Reserved' (administered by the Governor) and 'Transferred' (administered by the Governor with the aid of Ministers). Diarchy was only introduced at the Central level by the subsequent Government of India Act of 1935. II. is Correct. The Act of 1919 provided for the establishment of a Public Service Commission for recruiting civil servants in India. This was a response to the growing demand for the Indianisation of the civil services. Consequently, the Central Public Service Commission was set up in 1926 following the recommendations of the Lee Commission (1924). Further Insights: Bicameralism: The 1919 Act introduced for the first time a bicameral legislature at the Center, consisting of an Upper House (Council of State) and a Lower House (Legislative Assembly). Direct Elections: It introduced direct elections in the country for the first time, though the franchise was limited based on property, tax, or education. Communal Representation: The Act extended the principle of communal representation by providing separate electorates for Sikhs, Indian Christians, Anglo-Indians, and Europeans (expanding upon the 1909 Act which provided it for Muslims). High Commissioner: It created a new office of the High Commissioner for India in London and transferred to him some of the functions hitherto performed by the Secretary of State for India.

questions.question: In the context of the GST Council, what constitutes the 'quorum' for a meeting?
questions.explanation: The Quorum Rule: According to Article 279A of the Indian Constitution, the quorum for any meeting of the GST Council is one-half (50%) of its total members. This means at least 50% of the members (including the Union and State representatives) must be present for the Council to transact any business. Definition of Quorum: Quorum refers to the minimum number of members of a deliberative body necessary to conduct the business of that group and make proceedings valid. Further Insights: Decision Making: While the quorum is 50%, the decisions of the GST Council are taken by a majority of not less than three-fourths (75%) of the weighted votes of the members present and voting. Weightage of Votes: The Union Government has a weightage of one-third of the total votes cast, while all the State Governments combined have a weightage of two-thirds of the total votes cast in that meeting. Council Composition: The Council is chaired by the Union Finance Minister and includes the Union Minister of State in charge of Revenue or Finance and the Ministers in charge of Finance or Taxation of all the States.

questions.question: Which demographic indicator measures the number of deaths of children under one year of age per 1,000 live births?
questions.explanation: The demographic indicator that measures the number of deaths of children under one year of age per 1,000 live births is the Infant Mortality Rate (IMR). Explanation of Options: (a) is Incorrect. The Crude Death Rate (CDR) measures the total number of deaths in a particular population over a specific period (usually a year), expressed per 1,000 individuals. It is considered "crude" because it does not take into account the age or sex structure of the population. (b) is Correct. The Infant Mortality Rate (IMR) is a specific and critical health indicator. It calculates the probability of a child dying between birth and exactly one year of age. It is globally recognized as a sensitive proxy for the level of health, nutrition, and social development in a region. (c) is Incorrect. The Total Fertility Rate (TFR) represents the average number of children that would be born to a woman over her lifetime if she were to experience the current age-specific fertility rates through her reproductive years. It is used to measure population growth and replacement levels. (d) is Incorrect. Life Expectancy refers to the average number of years a person is expected to live based on current mortality rates. It is usually calculated at birth but can be determined for any specific age. Further Insights: Significance: IMR is more than just a demographic count; it reflects the availability of prenatal and postnatal care, the prevalence of infectious diseases, the status of maternal health, and the quality of sanitation and clean water. Indian Context: India has seen a significant decline in IMR over the last few decades due to initiatives like the National Health Mission (NHM) and the Universal Immunization Programme. According to the Sample Registration System (SRS) data, India's IMR dropped from 129 in 1971 to around 28 in recent years, though significant disparities remain between urban and rural areas and across different states (e.g., Kerala having a very low IMR compared to Madhya Pradesh). Related Metrics: Demographers also track Neonatal Mortality Rate (deaths within the first 28 days of life) and the Under-5 Mortality Rate (deaths before reaching the age of five) to get a comprehensive picture of child survival. Sustainable Development Goals (SDG): SDG 3 aims to end preventable deaths of newborns and children under 5 years of age by 2030, with all countries aiming to reduce neonatal mortality to at least as low as 12 per 1,000 live births and under-5 mortality to at least as low as 25 per 1,000 live births.

questions.question: Which of the following limestone features is formed by the continuous dripping of mineral-rich water from the ceiling to the floor?
questions.explanation: A stalagmite is the feature formed on the floor of a cave by mineral-rich water dripping from the ceiling. Explanation of Options: (a) is Incorrect. Stalactites are the icicle-like formations that hang "tite" from the ceiling of a cave. They are formed as water evaporates before it falls, leaving calcium carbonate behind. (b) is Incorrect. A Sinkhole is a surface feature. It is a depression or hole in the ground caused by the collapse of the surface layer, often due to the dissolution of limestone bedrock underneath. (c) is Correct. As water drops fall from the ceiling to the floor, they deposit small amounts of calcium carbonate. Over thousands of years, these deposits build upward to form a mound or pillar known as a Stalagmite. (d) is Incorrect. Lapies are a surface feature of Karst topography, characterized by grooved, fluted, or jagged ridges formed by the chemical weathering of limestone by rainwater. Further Insights: When a stalactite (growing down) and a stalagmite (growing up) eventually meet, they form a continuous structure known as a Pillar or Column. This type of landscape is known as Karst Topography, named after the Karst region in Slovenia where these limestone features are prominent.

questions.question: If cos φ = 15/17, then find the value of (2 cosec φ − 3 tan φ).
If 81⁽²ˣ ⁺ ³⁾ = 729ʸ, then which of the following is true?
Find the HCF of the fractions 5/12, 10/18 and 15/24.
questions.explanation: HCF of fractions = (HCF of numerators) / (LCM of denominators) HCF(5, 10, 15) = 5 LCM(12, 18, 24) = 72 Therefore, HCF = 5 / 72

questions.question: Two persons P and Q start from points A and B respectively, which are 200 km apart, heading towards each other. P travels at 10 km/h and Q at 20 km/h. After 4 hours of travel, Q stops for 30 minutes, then continues at the same speed. At what time from the start do they meet?
questions.explanation: Distance covered by P in 4 h = 10 X 4 = 40 km Distance covered by Q in 4 h = 20 X 4 = 80 km Remaining distance = 200 − (40+80) = 80 km During Q's stop of 0.5 h, P covers = 10 X 0.5 = 5 km New remaining distance = 80 − 5 = 75 km Relative speed when both moving = 10+20 = 30 km/h Time to meet after stop = 75/30 = 2.5 h Total time = 4 + 0.5 + 2.5 = 7 h

questions.question: The number of males in coaches A and B, are in the ratio of 3: 2. The number of males in coach C is 20% more than the sum of number of males in coaches A and B. If the number of males in coaches B and C together is 400, then find the number of males in coach A.
questions.explanation: Let number of males in coaches A and B be 3x and 2x respectively. Then, Number of males in coach C = 5x * 120/100 = 6x Given, 2x + 6x = 400 8x = 400 x = 50 Therefore, Number of males in coach A = 3x = 3 * 50 = 150

questions.question: The cost of a pearl is directly proportional to the square of its weight. The cost of a 25 grams pearl is ₹3000. This pearl got broken down into two pieces in the ratio of 3:2. How much loss percent is incurred due to this breakage?
questions.explanation: Cost of a pearl ∝ Square of its weight 3000 = k*25² k = 24/5 Now, Weight of pieces are ⅗ * 25 = 15 grams and ⅖ * 25= 10 grams Total cost = 24/5 * 15 * 15 + 24/5 * 10 * 10 = ₹1560 Loss percentage = [(3000 - 1560)/3000] * 100 = 48%

questions.question: If A + B = 90° and (1 + tan A)(1 + tan B) = 2n, what is n?

The salary of Ram is 75% of the salary of Laxman. If the salary of Ram increases by 40% and the salary of Laxman increases by 10%. By what percentage is the new salary of Laxman more than the new salary of Ram?
questions.explanation: Let the salary of Ram be Rs. a and Laxman be Rs. b According to question, a = 75% of b a = 3b/4 new salary of Ram = 140% of 3b/4 = 1.05b new salary of Laxman = 110% of b = 1.1b required percentage = (1.1b - 1.05b) * 100/(1.05b) = 4.76%

questions.question: 5% simple interest on first part and 7% simple interest on second yields Rs.360 in total, and the second sum is ₹1,200 more than the first. What is the total principal?

Amit bought a laptop for ₹20,000 and spent ₹4,000 on repairs. He sold it to Rohit at a profit of 25%. Rohit then spent ₹3,000 on upgrades and sold it to Suresh at a profit of ₹11000. Find the profit percentage earned by Rohit.
questions.explanation: Cost price of laptop for Amit = 20000 + 4000 = Rs. 24000 Cost price of laptop for Rohit = 125% of 24000 + 3000 = Rs. 33000 Profit % = 11000/33000 * 100 = 33.33%

questions.question: In the following figure, M and N are the midpoints. If QR - MN = 10 cm, then find the length of QR. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p22768/image036.png)
questions.explanation: Given that M and N are the midpoint so, MN = QR/2 And QR - MN = 10 QR - (QR/2) = 10 QR = 20 cm

Two numbers a and b are in the ratio of 3 : 7. When 9 is added to both the numbers the ratio becomes 15 : 29. Find the difference of a and b.
questions.explanation: Let the numbers a and b be 3x and 7x respectively, According to question, (3x + 9)/(7x + 9) = 15/29 87x + 261 = 105x + 135 18x = 126 x = 7 a = 21, b = 49 required difference = 49 - 21 = 28

questions.question: A trader bought 10 pens: five at ₹60 each and five at ₹90 each. He marks all pens at the same price and offers a flat 10% discount. If he desires an overall profit of 20%, what should be the common marked price per pen?
questions.explanation: Total cost = 5 X 60 + 5 X 90 = 300 + 450 = ₹750 Number of pens = 10 Average CP per pen = 750/10 = ₹75 Target SP per pen for 20% profit = 1.20 X 75 = ₹90 Let M = marked price per pen (₹) SP after 10% discount = 0.90M 0.90M = 90 M = 90 / 0.90 = ₹100

questions.question: Five years ago, the ratio of A's to B's ages was 4:7. Seven years from now, it will be 5:8. What is A's present age?
A and B invest Rs.100000 and Rs.150000. After 6 months, B withdraws Rs.50000. If profit is Rs.108000 for a year, what is B's share?

Workers A, B, C take 10, 15, and 20 days respectively to finish a job alone. They start together; after 2 days C leaves; after 3 more days B leaves; the remaining work is done by A. How many days did A work alone at the end?
questions.explanation: Rates: A=⅒, B=1/15, C=1/20. Work in 2 days = 2 X (⅒+1/15+1/20) = 2 X (13/60) = 13/30. Next 3 days work done by (A+B) = 3 X (⅒+1/15) = 3 X (⅙) = ½ = 15/30. Total work done so far = 13/30 + 15/30 = 28/30 = 14/15; Remaining work = 1 - (14/15) = 1/15. A's rate = ⅒; time = (1/15) ÷ (⅒) = 10/15 = ⅔ day.

questions.question: In a division sum, the divisor is six times the quotient and the quotient exceeds the remainder by two. If the divisor is 72, determine the remainder.
questions.explanation: Divisor = 72 Quotient = 72/6 = 12 Remainder = 12 - 2 = 10

questions.question: The intersection point of all the three interior angle bisectors of a triangle is called -
questions.explanation: The intersection point of all the three interior angle bisectors of a triangle is called incentre of the triangle.

questions.question: The distance between the point P ((3X - 2), 15) and the origin is 17 units. What is the value of X?
Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct speech. Prem said that he prayed for everyone's well being.
options: Prem said, "He prays for everyone's well being."
Prem said, "He will pray for everyone's well being."
Prem said, "He should pray for everyone's well being."
Prem said, "He had to pray for everyone's well being."
questions.explanation: The sentence in the Indirect Speech is in Simple Past Tense. Therefore, the sentence in the Direct Speech will be in Simple Present Tense. Simple Past (Subject +V2 + Object) will be converted into Simple Present (Subject +V1st + Object). For example: He said that he never went to the village. (Indirect Speech) He said, "I never go to the village." (Direct Speech) The following things in the sentence will change when changed from Indirect Speech to Direct Speech: Indirect SpeechDirect Speech said thatSaid prayedprays From the given options, one that follows the above mentioned conditions for Direct speech is option (a) "Prem said, "He prays for everyone's well being."" Hence, the correct answer is option (a).

questions.question: Find the synonym of "Meager".
questions.explanation: The word "meager" refers to something that is insufficient or scanty. (a) "Rich" is the opposite of "meager". (b) "Silent" relates to sound. (c) "Sparse" means thinly distributed or inadequate, matching "meager". (d) "Loyal" relates to faithfulness. Hence, "sparse" is the correct synonym.

questions.question: Identify the tense and voice used in the following sentence taken from the passage: "Emotions such as anger, frustration, anxiety, or excitement are natural human experiences."
questions.explanation: In the sentence, the verb "are" links the subject "Emotions" to the complement "natural human experiences." The structure is in the present simple tense and active voice, as the subject is not receiving an action but being described. There is no passive construction involved. Hence, (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: What grammatical function does the phrase "instead of reacting impulsively" serve in the following sentence? "Zen Mode encourages individuals to pause, observe, and respond thoughtfully instead of reacting impulsively."
questions.explanation: The phrase "instead of reacting impulsively" modifies the verb "respond," explaining how the action is carried out. It provides additional information about manner and contrast, not naming or describing a noun. Therefore, it functions as an adverbial phrase of manner. Option (a): It is not a noun phrase → Correct negation (the phrase does not act as a noun). Option (c) It is not an adjective phrase → Correct negation (it does not describe a noun). Option (d) It is not a gerund phrase functioning as subject → Correct negation (it is a gerund phrase, but it does not function as the subject of the sentence). Hence, (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Choose the word closest in meaning to the word "composure" as used in the passage.
questions.explanation: In the passage, "composure" refers to maintaining emotional control and calm behaviour, especially in challenging situations. Among the options, "calmness" most closely conveys this sense. The other options either suggest emotional distance or lack of flexibility, which are not intended here. Hence, (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Which of the following best expresses the central idea of the passage?
options: Zen Mode teaches emotional suppression to achieve peace and prosperity in life.
Emotional discipline through Zen Mode enhances personal and professional balance.
Emotional reactions are unavoidable in modern life and must not be paid attention to.
Professional success depends primarily on emotional neutrality and detachment.
questions.explanation: The passage consistently presents Zen Mode as a practice of emotional discipline that helps individuals regulate responses, improve relationships, and handle professional challenges effectively. It does not advocate emotional suppression or narrow professional outcomes. Therefore, option (b) best captures the central idea of the passage. Hence, (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Which of the following best captures the author's attitude towards emotional discipline as presented through Zen Mode?
options: It is portrayed as an impractical ideal in fast-paced modern life.
It is examined as a detached philosophical concept with limited application.
It is presented as a balanced and purposeful response to emotional reactivity.
It is depicted as a rigid framework that discourages emotional expression.
questions.explanation: The author consistently presents Zen Mode as a practical and mindful approach to managing emotions without suppressing them. Reference line: "Emotional discipline does not imply suppression of feelings or emotional detachment; rather, it refers to the conscious regulation of one's emotional responses". Emotional discipline is shown as intentional, reflective, and applicable across personal and professional contexts. The tone is neither dismissive nor abstract, nor does it promote rigidity. Therefore, option (c) best reflects the author's attitude. Hence, (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom. My best friend has stayed with me through thick and thin.
options: In both good and bad times
During short trips and long vacations
questions.explanation: The idiom "through thick and thin" describes a strong and enduring loyalty, indicating that someone supports you in all circumstances - both the good times and the bad times. In the given sentence, it suggests that the speaker's best friend has consistently been there for them, regardless of the good or bad times. Hence, option (b) is the answer.

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: Among the given words, option (d) 'compulsary' is the incorrectly spelt word. The correct spelling is 'compulsory', which means 'required by law or a rule; obligatory'. Hence, the correct answer is option (d). Immoral- not conforming to accepted standards of morality. Nominal- (of a role or status) existing in name only. Elective- related to or working by means of election.

questions.question: Select the appropriate option for the given blank. The building was built ________.
questions.explanation: The blank requires an adverb of manner to denote the way the building is built. Accordingly, option (d), 'hastily' is the only adverb of manner given in the option and so, it can fill the blank as required. 'Rough', 'complete' and 'swift' are adjectives and 'more' is an adverb of degree and so, these options are negated. The complete sentence is: The building was built hastily. Hence, the correct answer is option (d).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. The homophone for the word 'build' is ________.
questions.explanation: Homophones are words that sound the same but have different meanings and spellings. "Build" and "billed" are pronounced the same, but they have different meanings: Build - To construct something. Billed - Past tense of "bill," meaning to send or receive an invoice. Hence, option (c) is the answer. Built - The past tense and past participle of "build." It means something has been constructed or made. Bile - A digestive fluid produced by the liver, or metaphorically, it can mean anger or bitterness. Belt - A strip of material used to hold up clothing or as a mechanical device to transmit motion. It can also mean to hit something forcefully (e.g., "belted the ball").

questions.question: Select the most appropriate 'one word' for the expression given below. A place or scene of activity, debate or conflict.
questions.explanation: 'Arena' means a place or scene of activity, debate or conflict. Hence, (c) is the correct answer. 'Asylum' means an institution for the maintenance and care of persons requiring specialized assistance. 'Aquarium' means a tank often made of glass for keeping live fish. 'Aviary' means a birdhouse.

questions.question: Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error. If there is no error in the sentence, select 'No error'. I cracked SSC CGL with nothing else but determination and the guts to never give up.
options: I cracked SSC CGL with nothing else
questions.explanation: ·The sentence is grammatically correct. ·There is no error in any part of the sentence. ·Therefore, the answer is: (d) No error.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. Not in my (A)/ dreams I did (B)/ think that he (C)/ would betray me (D).
questions.explanation: Part B has an error. Inverted form of verb that is verb preceding the subject is used in sentence that begins with 'not'. In this sentence, the inverted form of verb is not used as required and it needs to be modified. The correct sentence is: Not in my dreams did I think that he would betray me. Hence, the correct answer is option (b).

questions.question: Identify the sentence that uses the positive degree of comparison.
options: The library's new reading room is quieter than the old one.
The library's new reading room is the quietest space on the entire campus.
The library's new reading room is more quiet than the study hall.
The library's new reading room is quiet and well-lit for all visitors.
questions.explanation: The correct sentence is option d). "Quiet" is in the positive degree, as it simply states the quality without comparison. Option a) uses the comparative form "quieter." Option b) uses the superlative form "quietest." Option c) incorrectly uses "more quiet" for a one-syllable adjective that typically forms the comparative by adding - er. Note: · The Positive Degree is used to denote the mere existence of some quality of what we speak about. It is used when no comparison is made. · The Comparative degree denotes a higher degree of quality than the positive and is used when two things are compared. · The Superlative degree indicates the highest degree of quality and is used when more than two things (or sets of things) are compared.

questions.question: Choose the sentence that uses a homonym of the underlined word: I saw the nail being hit on the wood.
options: These nails must be hit on the shaking furniture
He hung the picture with a single nail on the wall.
The metal nail bent under the heavy blow.
She filed her nail to a perfect shape.
questions.explanation: In the original sentence, "nails" denotes a small metal spike with a broadened flat head, driven into wood to join things together or to serve as a hook. Options a), b), and c) use "nailed" in the same sense. Option d) uses "nail" as a fingernail, which is unrelated to action. Hence, option (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. Hardly he imitate celebrities and politicians.
questions.explanation: The given sentence starts with the adverb 'hardly' (scarcely). When the sentence starts with an adverb, we always use subject-verb inversion [the verb is positioned before the subject]. So, the underlined part of the sentence is grammatically incorrect as the subject-verb inversion is not applied here. Option (a) is eliminated as it doesn't follow the subject-verb agreement. We should use the singular verb (imitates) with the singular subject 'he'. Option (b) is also eliminated as it doesn't follow the subject-verb inversion. Option (d) is the correct choice among the given options. Hence, option (d) is the correct answer. Note: Subject-Verb Agreement: Matching up singular or plural subjects with singular or plural forms of a verb is part of the process called agreement. This can be understood better by the examples given: - "She likes cooking." [singular subject and verb] -- "They like to cook vegetables." [plural subject and verb]

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. Does he keep his word?
options: Is his word kept by him?
Is his word to be kept by him?
Was his word kept by him?
questions.explanation: The subject of the Active Voice is changed into the object of the Passive Voice. Also, the object of the Active Voice is changed into the subject of the Passive Voice. The usage of a helping verb is necessary while changing the voice of the sentence from Active to Passive. The preposition "by" is used before the object in the passive voice. However, it is optional in some cases. The question in the active voice is an interrogative sentence. The answer to this question will be either "yes" or "no". These types of sentences usually begin with a helping verb like:Do Does The format of the sentence which begins with "Do" or "Does" in Active voice is: Do/ Does + Subject + V1 + Object? Therefore, in Passive voice, the format will be: Am/Is/Are + Subject (Objective case) + V3 + by + Object (Subjective case). For example: Does he need therapy? (Active) Is therapy needed by him? (Passive) The options except (a) don't follow the correct grammatical format of the Passive Voice. Thus, the correct answer is: (a) Is his word kept by him?

questions.question: Choose the word from the options that is opposite in meaning to the given word. Indigent
questions.explanation: 'Indigent' means 'Poor; needy.' Options a) and c) can be rejected as they are unrelated to the word. 'Impecunious' means 'Having little or no money.' Hence, option b) can also be rejected as it has the same meaning as the word. 'Furtive' means 'Attempting to avoid notice or attention, typically because of guilt or a belief that discovery would lead to trouble; secretive.' 'Abstain' means 'Restrain oneself from doing or enjoying something.' Hence, option d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Ferocious
questions.explanation: Ferocious/ savage - fierce. Grave/ solemn - serious. Benign - harmless. B is the right answer.

questions.question: Which spelling is correct for expressing disapproval?
questions.explanation: "Pejorative" means expressing contempt or disapproval; having a negative or belittling connotation. Usage: The term was once neutral but has now taken on a pejorative meaning in modern usage. Hence, option (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Chip off the old block
options: the son is like the father
to lose a lot of money
questions.explanation: The idiom 'chip off the old block' means that the son is similar to the father. E.g. As soon as I learnt that Roger is good at painting like his father, I thought to myself that he was a chip off the old block. B is the right answer.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. A person who renounces material comforts
questions.explanation: An "ascetic" is someone who practices severe self-discipline and abstains from worldly pleasures, often for spiritual or religious reasons. A "sybarite" is a person who loves expensive things. A "monk" is a person who dedicates their life to religious service, often living in a monastery. A "cynic" is a person who believes that people are only interested in themselves and are not sincere. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM to replace the underlined word. Toxic workplaces impose serious and lasting harm on affected employees.
questions.explanation: Lasting: enduring or able to endure over a long period of time. Ephemeral: something that lasts for a very short time. So, the correct option is 'ephemeral' (option (c)). Surviving: remaining alive, especially after the death of another or others. Enduring: lasting over a period of time; durable. Undying: (especially of an emotion) lasting forever.

questions.question: Parts of a sentence are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the parts in the correct order to form a meaningful sentence. O. of being posted P. the creative team was thrilled Q. when their latest viral reel R. gained millions of views S. within just a few hours
questions.explanation: After reading all the parts thoroughly, we can say that pairing P with Q logically starts the sentence with "the creative team was thrilled when their latest viral reel," introducing the subject and their excitement. Next, pairing Q with R continues the thought by stating that the reel "gained millions of views," which elaborates on the reason for their thrill. Following this, R pairs well with S, as it specifies "within just a few hours," providing a time frame that emphasizes the speed of the reel's success. Finally, O concludes the sentence with "of being posted," giving context to the timeframe of the reel's viral status. Therefore, the correct order is PQRSO, making (a) the correct answer. The final sentence: The creative team was thrilled when their latest viral reel gained millions of views within just a few hours of being posted.
